opening_sequence.title: Welcome to the Citadel, Governor
opening_sequence.intro: Erbil stands as an oasis of stability, but it is a mirage. You are caught in a deadly balancing act between four masters: Baghdad controls the federal budget, Ankara bombs the borders, Tehran launches proxy missiles, and Washington is slowly losing interest. Survive the geopolitics while keeping the lights on.

advisors.name: Azad Sorani
advisors.hidden_agenda: Smuggling unregulated oil to Turkish buyers to bypass Baghdad's oversight, taking a massive personal cut.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Handing full control and auditing of the oil pipelines back to the federal Iraqi government.

briefing: Baghdad has delayed our budget share for the fourth month in a row.
The civil servants are threatening to strike if they don't get paid by Friday.
We have 'unofficial' buyers for the surplus crude, if you look the other way.
agreement: A very profitable choice.
The black market thrives on this kind of pragmatism.
disagreement: You are leaving millions on the table.
Baghdad won't reward you for your honesty, Governor.
dialogue.betrayal: You sold our financial independence back to Baghdad. I've taken my ledgers and my wealth to Dubai.
roast: Your economic strategy is essentially begging Baghdad for our own money.
I've seen street vendors in the bazaar with better financial foresight.
scandal_reaction: Burn the secondary ledgers immediately.
Blame the federal government for the missing funds.

advisors.name: Shilan Mahmoud
advisors.hidden_agenda: Channeling Gulf reconstruction contracts to companies owned by her husband's family.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Mandating an independent, transparent international bidding process for all infrastructure deals above $5 million.

briefing: The UAE and Qatar are ready to pour money into Erbil – but they want guaranteed returns.
We can rebuild the airport road in six months with the right private partner.
Baghdad won't give us a dinar. The Gulf will – at a price.
agreement: Fast capital is better than no capital.
The skyline will thank you in two years.
disagreement: You're turning us into a Gulf colony. Where's the local ownership?
Transparency sounds noble until no one bids.
dialogue.betrayal: You opened the bids. Now my family's consortium lost everything. The Gulf knows your name – and they're not happy.
roast: Your investment strategy is a post-it note that says 'hope'.
I've seen more financial discipline in a casino.
scandal_reaction: Blame the corruption on the previous minister. Always works.
Freeze the accounts and say nothing. I'll handle the whispers.

advisors.name: General Sherzad
advisors.hidden_agenda: Purging rival political party commanders from the joint Peshmerga brigades to consolidate personal power.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Integrating the Peshmerga fully under the command of the Iraqi Armed Forces.

briefing: Turkish drones are circling the northern mountain villages again.
Iranian-backed militias are massing near the Kirkuk checkpoint.
The men need ammunition, not more political speeches.
agreement: For the blood of the martyrs.
A strong defense is our only diplomacy.
disagreement: You are making us look weak to our enemies.
The mountains are our only true friends, don't forget that.
dialogue.betrayal: You handed our guns and our honor to Baghdad. The Peshmerga will never follow you again.
roast: You govern like a man who has never heard a gunshot.
You would surrender the Citadel for a pat on the head from Washington.
scandal_reaction: Deploy the elite units to the streets. No protests.
Keep the men in the barracks until this blows over.

advisors.name: Colonel Hana Khoshnaw
advisors.hidden_agenda: Using Peshmerga intelligence assets to run a private assassination squad against political opponents.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Ordering a complete audit of all Zanyari intelligence agency off-book operations.

briefing: ISI sleeper cells are active in the refugee camps. I have names.
Turkey is sharing drone feeds with us – but they also record our movements.
One targeted strike tonight can break the militia network in Makhmour.
agreement: Silent and surgical. I like it.
The enemy won't see this coming.
disagreement: You're asking for proof while bombs are being built. That's how we lose.
Soft intelligence gets agents killed. Hard choices save lives.
dialogue.betrayal: Your audit would have exposed my network. So I exposed yours – to the Turkish consulate.
roast: You couldn't run a surveillance op on a parked car.
Your security briefings are just newspaper headlines with coffee stains.
scandal_reaction: Classify the incident. Burn the source logs. Deny everything.
This is why we don't keep written records of certain extractions.

advisors.name: Niyan Jaff
advisors.hidden_agenda: Re-routing foreign luxury investment to build permanent housing for neglected IDP camps on the outskirts.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Bulldozing a major Syrian refugee camp to build a luxury mega-mall for foreign expats.

briefing: The winter is coming and the camps are lacking basic heating oil.
Foreign oil executives want more luxury compounds in Ankawa.
We are building a Dubai-style skyline while our people freeze in tents.
agreement: Finally, some humanity in this office.
The displaced families will bless your name.
disagreement: You only care about the skyline, not the people in its shadow.
This is a betrayal of our most vulnerable.
dialogue.betrayal: You destroyed their last refuge for a shopping mall. I am taking the NGOs and going to the international press.
roast: Your legacy will be empty glass towers and broken promises.
You sold Erbil's soul to the highest bidder.
scandal_reaction: This is what happens when you prioritize greed.
I will not defend this administration's morals.

advisors.name: Rebin Tofiq
advisors.hidden_agenda: Privatizing the region's water grid to sell licenses to a Turkish hydro corporation.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Declaring water a free public good protected by a new constitutional amendment.

briefing: The dams upstream in Turkey are strangling our river flow.
We lose 40% of our treated water to leaks in pipes installed in the 1980s.
Solar-powered desalination is expensive, but it's the only drought-proof option.
agreement: Efficiency over sentiment. Water is a commodity.
Private capital fixes pipes faster than public promises.
disagreement: You're selling drinking water to the highest bidder. That's medieval.
Privatization without regulation is just legalized theft.
dialogue.betrayal: You declared water free. My Turkish partners have pulled out – and so have I. Enjoy the droughts.
roast: Your infrastructure plan is 'pray for rain.' Inspiring.
I've seen better pipe maintenance in a Roman ruin.
scandal_reaction: Blame the Turkish dams. Nationalist anger covers everything.
Release the emergency reserves and hide the contract.

advisors.name: Kamaran Barzani
advisors.hidden_agenda: Controlling the lucrative Ibrahim Khalil border crossing tariffs exclusively for his own clan.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Inviting federal Iraqi border guards to permanently manage the international crossings.

briefing: The Turkish border is backed up for miles with commercial trucks.
Smugglers are bypassing the official checkpoints in the mountains.
The airport radar system needs an upgrade before the FAA bans flights.
agreement: The goods will flow smoothly.
My people at the border will appreciate this.
disagreement: You are choking our supply lines.
Don't blame me when the bazaars run out of food.
dialogue.betrayal: You handed the border to Baghdad. You just cut my family's throat. Watch your back.
roast: You couldn't smuggle a pack of cigarettes across a bridge.
This administration is running on empty.
scandal_reaction: I have a flight ready for you if things get too hot.
Keep the border open, no matter what the press says.

advisors.name: Dilan Omar
advisors.hidden_agenda: Awarding bus and taxi permits exclusively to companies owned by his in-laws.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Creating an open, transparent mobile app for real-time public transit licensing and bidding.

briefing: Traffic in Erbil has tripled in five years. No one can get to work on time.
The old minibuses are unsafe. But new ones cost money we don't have.
A unified payment card system could reduce bribery at checkpoints.
agreement: Move people, move the economy. Simple math.
The streets will breathe again.
disagreement: Open bidding means mainland Iraqi companies. They'll low-ball and disappear.
Transparency sounds good until your aunt's bus company goes bankrupt.
dialogue.betrayal: You opened the permit app. Now my family's fleet is idle. Enjoy the traffic jams – they're your fault now.
roast: Your transit strategy is 'buy more SUVs.' Brilliant.
I've seen faster commutes in a funeral procession.
scandal_reaction: Blame the traffic on the refugees. Easy target.
Announce a new ring road. Never build it. Problem delayed.

advisors.name: Mullah Karwan
advisors.hidden_agenda: Establishing conservative Islamic dress codes in public spaces to suppress secular opposition.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Sponsoring a massive, internationally-televised secular music and arts festival at the Citadel.

briefing: The Friday sermons are growing hostile towards the government's corruption.
Western NGOs are introducing concepts that conflict with our traditions.
We must balance our rapid modernization with spiritual groundedness.
agreement: Allah will reward your wisdom.
This protects the moral fabric of Kurdistan.
disagreement: You are inviting Western decadence into our homes.
The faithful will not forget this insult.
dialogue.betrayal: You have turned the Citadel into a den of secular sin. I declare your government illegitimate.
roast: You seek approval from Washington while losing the approval of Heaven.
Your moral compass is guided entirely by foreign aid dollars.
scandal_reaction: This is the inevitable result of abandoning our values.
We must pray for forgiveness for the city.

advisors.name: Father Aram Korkis
advisors.hidden_agenda: Securing Vatican-mediated protection for Assyrian Christian villages in the Nineveh Plain.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Signing a land-swap deal with Turkey that includes Christian-majority towns.

briefing: Our churches are empty. The young have fled to Germany and Sydney.
Muslim and Christian co-existence is a fact here – but outside forces want to divide us.
The Vatican is willing to invest in Christian schools if we guarantee safety.
agreement: A just path. The cross and the crescent can share the sky.
Our ancient bloodlines will thank you.
disagreement: You're trading Christian land for Turkish drones. That's betrayal dressed as diplomacy.
Minority rights are not bargaining chips.
dialogue.betrayal: You signed away our villages. The Vatican has excommunicated you in all but name. Goodbye.
roast: You protect every group except the ones who were here first.
Your 'religious tolerance' is a press release written by your PR firm.
scandal_reaction: Call the bishop. Then call CNN. This is our moment.
The martyrs are watching. Do not lie to them.

decisions.title: The Baghdad Budget Freeze
decisions.body: The federal government in Baghdad has frozen our share of the national budget. Civil servants and Peshmerga haven't been paid in three months, and strikes are looming.

options.label: Cut municipal services heavily to scrape together partial salaries.

consequences.public_reaction: Half a salary is better than starving, but the trash is piling up.

options.label: Sell oil independently to Turkey, defying Baghdad and angering Tehran.

consequences.public_reaction: We don't need Baghdad! Kurdistan can survive on its own!

options.label: Print unofficial local KRG currency and force local merchants to accept it.

consequences.public_reaction: This paper is worthless! You are destroying our life savings!

decisions.title: Drone Strikes on the Border
decisions.body: Turkish drones are striking targets in the northern mountains, ostensibly targeting PKK militants, but they just killed civilians in a local farming village.

options.label: Issue a formal, toothless condemnation to Ankara to maintain trade relations.

consequences.public_reaction: You value Turkish tomatoes more than Kurdish blood.

options.label: Deploy Peshmerga to block Turkish military advances, risking open conflict.

consequences.public_reaction: Finally, we stand up to the incursions!

options.label: Secretly arm the mountain militants to bleed the Turkish forces.

consequences.public_reaction: Dangerous game. If Ankara finds out, they will level the city.

decisions.title: The Expats and the Citadel
decisions.body: Western oil executives want to privatize a historic section of the Erbil Citadel to build a heavily fortified luxury compound.

options.label: Decline the offer and keep the Citadel fully public.

consequences.public_reaction: The Citadel belongs to the people, not to foreign oil men.

options.label: Approve the luxury compound for a massive, much-needed cash injection.

consequences.public_reaction: We are selling our history to people who can't even speak our language.

options.label: Seize the expats' current properties in Ankawa and move IDPs into them.

consequences.public_reaction: The consulates are evacuating, but the refugees finally have a roof!

decisions.title: Militia Blockade
decisions.body: Iranian-backed PMF militias in neighboring Kirkuk are threatening to blockade the main commercial highway into Erbil unless we pay them a 'transit tax'.

options.label: Request US coalition forces to patrol the highway.

consequences.public_reaction: Relying on the Americans again. Let's hope they don't abandon us this time.

options.label: Pay off the militia commanders quietly with municipal funds.

consequences.public_reaction: We are paying extortion to our enemies.

options.label: Send an elite hit squad to assassinate the militia commander.

consequences.public_reaction: A strong message, but Tehran will not let this go unanswered.

decisions.title: Syrian Refugee Integration
decisions.body: A massive influx of Kurdish refugees fleeing Syria demands the right to work locally, angering unemployed Erbil residents who say jobs are already scarce.

options.label: Keep them confined to the camps with basic UN aid.

consequences.public_reaction: We have to look after our own first.

options.label: Grant them full working rights and absorb the local anger.

consequences.public_reaction: They are our brothers and sisters, but they are driving down wages.

options.label: Force them onto buses and send them toward the European borders.

consequences.public_reaction: We are treating fellow Kurds like cattle. Shame on us.

crises.name: Missile Strike on Suburbs
crises.trigger: The IRGC has fired ballistic missiles into an affluent Erbil suburb, claiming it was a foreign spy base.
crises.description: Massive destruction in the civilian sector. Mass casualties. The US coalition forces nearby did not intercept the missiles. The public is terrified and demanding protection we cannot provide.

options.label: Downplay the attack publicly while begging Washington for Patriot missile batteries.
Halt all Iranian imports and expel their diplomats, risking an economic embargo.
Launch a retaliatory suicide drone strike on an Iranian proxy base in Kirkuk.

crises.name: Export Pipeline Blown
crises.trigger: Unknown actors have blown up the main oil export pipeline to Ceyhan, cutting off 80% of regional revenue.
crises.description: The economy is in instant freefall. We cannot export oil. The treasury will be completely empty in less than a week. Baghdad is offering 'help' with extremely heavy strings attached.

options.label: Take out predatory, high-interest loans from Gulf states to survive the month.
Hand emergency repair and total operational control over to Baghdad for a bailout.
Confiscate the personal wealth of the top 100 Kurdish billionaires.

crises.name: Streets Erupt in Factional Violence
crises.trigger: Long-simmering political tensions between the region's main ruling families have erupted into open street fighting.
crises.description: Armed partisan forces are exchanging gunfire in the city center. Civilians are hiding in their homes. If this escalates into a full civil war, the region's autonomy will be destroyed from the inside.

options.label: Invite UN mediators immediately and impose a strict, city-wide curfew.
Mobilize your own loyalist forces to violently crush the rival faction.
Ask the federal Iraqi army to march into Erbil and restore order.

crises.name: The Four Masters Ultimatum
crises.trigger: Ankara, Tehran, Baghdad, and Washington have all issued conflicting ultimatums regarding Erbil's autonomy.
crises.description: Baghdad demands the airport. Turkey demands the border. Iran demands the expulsion of dissidents. The US is offering evacuation flights for expats. We cannot appease everyone. We must choose a master or fight them all.

options.label: Capitulate entirely to Baghdad, sacrificing political autonomy for survival and salaries.
Declare full, immediate independence, triggering blockades and inevitable war.
Sign a secret, asymmetrical defense pact with a rogue state to secure a nuclear umbrella.

scandals.title: Governor's Son Caught Smuggling Luxury Cars
scandals.description: Border guards arrested your son attempting to smuggle stolen luxury SUVs across the border, bypassing customs duties completely.

scandals.title: Millions Paid to 'Ghost' Peshmerga
scandals.description: An international audit revealed that 20% of the troops on the government payroll don't exist, and the money was routed to your party's accounts.

scandals.title: Governor Implicated in Selling Intel to Foreign Neighbors
scandals.description: Leaked audio recordings verify you provided the coordinates of opposition dissidents to a neighboring country's intelligence agency prior to a drone strike.

positive: At least the streets of Erbil are still safe.
We survived Saddam, we will survive this.
Good to see someone putting our people first.
A pragmatic choice in a terrible neighborhood.
neutral: Just more politicians getting rich while we wait for salaries.
Inshallah the budget will come this month.
Let's see what Baghdad says about this.
Another day, another crisis in the Middle East.
As long as the bazaar is open, life goes on.
We are pawns on a very large chessboard.
negative: Thieves and traitors, all of them.
They sold our blood for oil money.
I'm paying smugglers to get me to Europe tomorrow.
We have no electricity, no water, and no future here.
You are turning us into a proxy state!
media: Rudaw: Regional Government announces controversial new policy.
Kurdistan24: Baghdad responds aggressively to Erbil's latest move.
NRT: Protests erupt over unpaid civil servant salaries.
Al Jazeera: Tensions escalate on the northern Iraqi border.
Local News: Traffic paralyzed in Ankawa due to security checkpoints.
Breaking: Unidentified drones sighted over the capital.
politician: We will not compromise our hard-fought autonomy.
The federal government must fulfill its constitutional duties.
This is a betrayal of the blood of our martyrs.
We urge our neighbors to respect our sovereignty.
The opposition demands transparency on the oil revenues.
We are a force for stability in a chaotic region.
activist: Pay our teachers! We cannot feed our children on patriotism!
We demand an end to the two-party monopoly!
Protect the IDPs, they have nowhere else to go.
End the corruption that is bleeding this region dry.
We want a civilian government, not a militia state.
street: Kak, the generator fuel is too expensive this month.
Are the salaries coming this week or next month?
The checkpoint to Kirkuk is closed again.
Did you hear the explosion last night?
Can't get a job unless you have party connections.
Drinking chai and waiting for the end of the world.
crisis: To xwa, where are the Americans now?!
Everyone to the shelters, they are hitting the city!
The banks are empty! The economy is dead!
May God curse those who bring war to our homes.
Pack your bags, we have to flee to the mountains again.
This is the end of our autonomy.
scandal: Aib (Shame)! They steal while we starve.
We shed blood for this flag, and they use it to hide their money.
Bring them to justice in the bazaar.
They are worse than the regimes we fought.
I knew it. They are all working for the neighbors.
There is no honor left in politics.

scandal_reactions.minor: Everyone knows the politicians' kids do this. Nothing new.
scandal_reactions.moderate: Stealing from the Peshmerga is unforgivable. People are furious.
scandal_reactions.major: Treason! You sold our brothers to the enemy!
scandal_reactions.career_ending: The tribes and the parties will not protect you. Flee the country.

romance_exposure.flavour: In a region dominated by tribal honor and strict religious norms, this completely delegitimizes your moral authority and gives your political rivals the perfect excuse to oust you.
